topic: Tracking 80 leave balances in a shared spreadsheet — someone always gets the wrong number
format: Write a numbered list of 4-5 warning signs — make them specific enough that most readers recognise at least three of them
post:
80 employees. One shared spreadsheet. Zero confidence the numbers are right.

1. Someone asks for their leave balance — you open the file, scroll through 80 rows, and still aren't sure what to tell them.
2. Two managers edited it on the same day. Now there are two versions and neither is dated.
3. An employee took 3 days in March. Nobody updated the sheet. You found out during their annual review.
4. A staff member disputed their balance. You couldn't prove the correct figure. You gave in.
5. Someone is on leave right now and you're not fully sure it was ever formally approved.

This isn't really a spreadsheet problem.

It's a trust problem. When the records are unreliable, every leave conversation becomes a negotiation. HR chases numbers instead of doing actual work. Employees start questioning the process. And payroll can't close cleanly because nothing quite lines up.

One shared file with no audit trail isn't a system. It's a liability — and it usually surfaces at the worst possible time.

SmartPro Hub tracks every balance automatically, logs every request, and keeps one version of the truth — visible to HR, managers, and employees in real time.
